title: NIS code

infobox.value: ([Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"): NIS-code, French: code INS) is a numeric [code](/wiki/Code "Code") for regional areas of [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium").

number identifies the province. if this digit is followed by 4 zeroes, this code identifies the complete province. Example: 70000 identifies the province [Limburg](/wiki/Limburg_(Belgium) "Limburg (Belgium)").

digit identifies the [arrondissement](/wiki/Arrondissement "Arrondissement") within this province. If after the two first digits there are three zeroes, then this code identifies the complete arrondissement. Example: 71000 identifies the [arrondissement of Hasselt](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Hasselt "Arrondissement of Hasselt").
digits uniquely identify the [municipality](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Belgium "Municipalities of Belgium") within that arrondissement. Example: 71066 identifies [Zonhoven](/wiki/Zonhoven "Zonhoven").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/14/Gemeinden_Belgiens_2019.png/250px-Gemeinden_Belgiens_2019.png)](/wiki/File:Gemeinden_Belgiens_2019.png)

areas of Belgium

The **NIS code** ([Dutch](/wiki/Dutch_language "Dutch language"): NIS-code, French: code INS) is a numeric [code](/wiki/Code "Code") for regional areas of [Belgium](/wiki/Belgium "Belgium").

This code is used for statistical data treatment in Belgium.
This code was developed mid-1960s by the [Statistics Belgium](/wiki/Statistics_Belgium "Statistics Belgium"). It was first used for the census of 1970.

## Structure of the code

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=NIS_code&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Structure of the code")]

The NIS code consists of 5 digits:

* The **first** number identifies the province. if this digit is followed by 4 zeroes, this code identifies the complete province. Example: 70000 identifies the province [Limburg](/wiki/Limburg_(Belgium) "Limburg (Belgium)").
* The **second** digit identifies the [arrondissement](/wiki/Arrondissement "Arrondissement") within this province. If after the two first digits there are three zeroes, then this code identifies the complete arrondissement. Example: 71000 identifies the [arrondissement of Hasselt](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Hasselt "Arrondissement of Hasselt").
* The **last three** digits uniquely identify the [municipality](/wiki/Municipalities_of_Belgium "Municipalities of Belgium") within that arrondissement. Example: 71066 identifies [Zonhoven](/wiki/Zonhoven "Zonhoven").

## Special cases

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=NIS_code&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Special cases")]

* The country Belgium received the code 01000.
* The three regions received the codes 02000 for [Flanders](/wiki/Flanders "Flanders"), 03000 for [Wallonia](/wiki/Wallonia "Wallonia") and 04000 for the Brussels region.
* In 1995 the [province of Brabant](/wiki/Province_of_Brabant "Province of Brabant") with first digit 2 was split in [Flemish Brabant](/wiki/Flemish_Brabant "Flemish Brabant") and [Walloon Brabant](/wiki/Walloon_Brabant "Walloon Brabant"). Flemish Brabant received code 20001 and Walloon Brabant received code 20002. The arrondissements kept their old codes.
* The provinces and municipalities of Brussels are sorted alphabetically on their French name.

## Mergers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=NIS_code&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Mergers")]

* municipalities that were [merged](/wiki/Merger_(politics) "Merger (politics)") and received a new name also received a new NIS code, which followed the last number of the list of municipalities of that arrondissement.
* municipalities that lost their independence by merger and became municipality parts, also lost their NIS code. Per merged municipality only 1 NIS code remained. At the same time the structure of the NIS sector was adapted. An alphabetic letter was added per municipality part to be able to uniquely identify such a municipality part.
* The [Arrondissement of Brussels-Periphery](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Brussels-Periphery "Arrondissement of Brussels-Periphery") with code 22000 merging in 1971 with the [arrondissement of Halle-Vilvoorde](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Halle-Vilvoorde "Arrondissement of Halle-Vilvoorde"), also lost its code by merger. The municipalities within this arrondissement received a new NIS code.

## Examples

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=NIS_code&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Examples")]

40000: Province [East Flanders](/wiki/East_Flanders "East Flanders")

* 4 Province East Flanders
* 0000 This code identifies the complete province

32000: [Arrondissement of Diksmuide](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Diksmuide "Arrondissement of Diksmuide")

* 3 Province [West Flanders](/wiki/West_Flanders "West Flanders")
* 2 Arrondissement of Diksmuide
* 000 This code identifies the complete arrondissement

73032: municipality [Hoeselt](/wiki/Hoeselt "Hoeselt")

* 7 Province [Limburg](/wiki/Limburg_(Belgium) "Limburg (Belgium)")
* 3 [Arrondissement of Tongeren](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Tongeren "Arrondissement of Tongeren")
* 032 municipality Hoeselt

61012: municipality [Clavier](/wiki/Clavier,_Li%C3%A8ge "Clavier, Liège")

* 6 Province [Liège](/wiki/Li%C3%A8ge_(province) "Liège (province)")
* 1 [Arrondissement of Huy](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Huy "Arrondissement of Huy")
* 012 municipality Clavier

23105: municipality [Affligem](/wiki/Affligem "Affligem")

* 23 [Arrondissement of Halle-Vilvoorde](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Halle-Vilvoorde "Arrondissement of Halle-Vilvoorde")
* 105 municipality Affligem (new name of this municipality, resulting in the number no longer corresponding with the alphabetic order)

71022E: municipality part [Stokrooie](/wiki/Stokrooie "Stokrooie")

* 7 Province Limburg
* 1 [Arrondissement of Hasselt](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Hasselt "Arrondissement of Hasselt")
* 022 Merged municipality [Hasselt](/wiki/Hasselt "Hasselt")
* E municipality part Stokrooie, which had the code 71056 before the merger.

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=NIS_code&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: External links")]

* [List of all NIS codes (2025)](https://statbel.fgov.be/sites/default/files/Over_Statbel_FR/Nomenclaturen/REFNIS_2025.csv)
